TEXT MAP v2 — two ALIGNED layers, one grid frame, north-up. 1 cell ≈ 2.1m. col 0..47 = W→E, row 0..27 = N→S (row 0 = north). Cells are space-separated (one symbol = one cell).
GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1177403 + (col+0.5)*2.3524e-5;  lat = 51.5239897 - (row+0.5)*2.3723e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1410035 + (col+0.5)*2.4243e-5;  lat = 51.5173220 - (row+0.5)*2.8032e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

TEXT MAP v2 — two ALIGNED layers, one grid frame, north-up. 1 cell ≈ 5.1m. col 0..47 = W→E, row 0..27 = N→S (row 0 = north). Cells are space-separated (one symbol = one cell).
GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1374455 + (col+0.5)*6.4336e-5;  lat = 51.4920002 - (row+0.5)*5.1264e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1400757 + (col+0.5)*4.6106e-5;  lat = 51.5176566 - (row+0.5)*3.4459e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1385753 + (col+0.5)*4.3450e-5;  lat = 51.5184960 - (row+0.5)*4.9093e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1339855 + (col+0.5)*3.4879e-5;  lat = 51.4924847 - (row+0.5)*3.3382e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

TEXT MAP v2 — two ALIGNED layers, one grid frame, north-up. 1 cell ≈ 2.9m. col 0..47 = W→E, row 0..27 = N→S (row 0 = north). Cells are space-separated (one symbol = one cell).
GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1477845 + (col+0.5)*3.5040e-5;  lat = 51.5140760 - (row+0.5)*3.0757e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1439169 + (col+0.5)*3.6987e-5;  lat = 51.4902023 - (row+0.5)*4.8761e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1472650 + (col+0.5)*2.4395e-5;  lat = 51.5143092 - (row+0.5)*3.2634e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GRID REF — cell(col,row) centre = [lng, lat]:  lng = -0.1331709 + (col+0.5)*5.9032e-5;  lat = 51.4938663 - (row+0.5)*6.3218e-5
CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

CROSS-REFERENCE: the layers share coordinates. Look up the SAME (col,row) in both layers: an equipment marker over '#' in LAYER 1 sits INSIDE that building; a cable glyph over '#' crosses that building — UNLESS that building is the cable's own source or target (a drop legitimately ENDS inside the building it serves; check CABLES source -> target).
HOW TO READ (hypothetical example, NOT from this scene): if LAYER 2 shows marker 'q' at (3,1) and LAYER 1 at (3,1) shows '#', that equipment sits INSIDE that building; '=' or '|' there means it sits on a street; ':' or '.' means open ground. The LEGEND's inside= field precomputes building containment exactly for every equipment item.
GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

GEOMETRY vs TOPOLOGY: the grids show WHERE things are (geometry). WHO connects to whom (topology) is in the LEGEND — each closure's serves= list and the CABLES section's source -> target. For connectivity/path questions, read the LEGEND; do not trace glyphs.

Consider building B-0. Its "home street" is the street nearest to it. Which closure is nearest to B-0 among the closures whose OWN nearest street is a DIFFERENT street than B-0's home street? Fill `closureId` with its id, or 'none' if every closure sits on the home street.
List the ids of every equipment item whose point lies INSIDE a building footprint. Fill `equipmentIds` (empty array if none).
List the ids of every cable whose path passes THROUGH a building footprint it does not terminate at. Fill `cableIds` (empty array if none).
Is equipment CL-A placed on a street (within ~8m of a street centerline), as opposed to off-street / inside a building? Fill `onStreet` (true/false).
Which closure is geographically nearest to building B-0? Fill `closureId` with its id.
Is there any building with NO closure within 35m of it (a coverage gap)? Fill `buildingIds` with every such building (empty array if none).
List the equipment on the path from building B-0 to the source (the CO), nearest-first. Fill `equipmentPath` with the ordered ids.
If a straight cable runs from CO-1 to building B-11, list the ids of every OTHER building whose footprint the straight line passes through (exclude B-11). Fill `buildingIds` (empty array if none).
